children.label: i
children.text: food service programs carried out with assistance under this chapter and, to the maximum extent practicable, using individuals who administer exemplary local food service programs in the State;

children.label: ii
children.text: school breakfast programs carried out with assistance under ; andsection 1773 of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: as appropriate, other federally assisted feeding programs; and

children.label: i
children.text: addresses the cost savings and improvements in program integrity and operations that would result from the use of new or upgraded technology;

children.label: ii
children.text: ensures that there is not any overt identification of any child by special tokens or tickets, announced or published list of names, or by any other means;

children.label: iii
children.text: provides for processing and verifying applications for free and reduced price school meals;

children.label: iv
children.text: integrates menu planning, production, and serving data to monitor compliance with ; andsection 1758(f)(1) of this title

children.label: v
children.text: establishes compatibility with statewide reporting systems;

children.label: i
children.text: participate in the school breakfast program under ; andsection 1773 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: demonstrate the greatest need, for the purpose of aiding schools in meeting costs associated with initiating or expanding a school breakfast program under , including outreach and informational activities; andsection 1773 of this title

from amounts appropriated pursuant to subsection (e)(2), is authorized to provide financial and other assistance to the University of Mississippi, in cooperation with the University of Southern Mississippi, to establish and maintain a food service management institute.
menu planning;
implementation of regulations and appropriate guidelines; and
compliance with program requirements and accountability for program operations.

children.heading: Duties of food service management institute
children.label: c Duties of food service management institute
A
children.text: food service programs assisted under this chapter;

children.label: B
children.text: school breakfast programs assisted under ; andsection 1773 of this title

children.label: C
children.text: as appropriate, other federally assisted feeding programs.

children.label: A
children.text: conducting research necessary to assist schools and other organizations that participate in such programs in providing high quality, nutritious, cost-effective meal service to the children served;

children.label: i
children.text: efficient use of physical resources;

children.label: ii
children.text: financial management;

children.label: iii
children.text: efficient use of computers;

children.label: iv
children.text: procurement;

children.label: v
children.text: sanitation;

children.label: vi
children.text: safety, including food handling, hazard analysis and critical control point plan implementation, emergency readiness, responding to a food recall, and food biosecurity training;

children.label: vii
children.text: meal planning and related nutrition activities;

children.label: viii
children.text: culinary skills; and

children.label: ix
children.text: other appropriate activities;

children.label: C
children.text: establishing a national network of trained professionals to present training programs and workshops for food service personnel;

children.label: D
children.text: developing training materials for use in the programs and workshops described in subparagraph (C);

children.label: E
children.text: acting as a clearinghouse for research, studies, and findings concerning all aspects of the operation of food service programs;

children.label: F
children.text: training food service personnel to comply with the nutrition guidance and objectives established by the Secretary through a national network of instructors or other means;

children.label: G
children.text: preparing informational materials, such as video instruction tapes and menu planners, to promote healthier food preparation; and

children.label: H
children.text: assisting State educational agencies in providing additional nutrition and health instructions and instructors, including training personnel to comply with the nutrition guidance and objectives established by the Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall coordinate activities carried out and assistance provided as required by subsection (b) with activities carried out by any food service management institute established as authorized by subsection (a)(2).

children.heading: Use of institute for dietary and nutrition activities
children.label: 2 Use of institute for dietary and nutrition activities
children.text: The Secretary shall use any food service management institute established under subsection (a)(2) to assist in carrying out dietary and nutrition activities of the Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In addition to any amounts otherwise made available for fiscal year 2011, on , and each October 1 thereafter, out of any funds in the Treasury not otherwise appropriated, the Secretary of the Treasury shall transfer to the Secretary to carry out subsection (a)(2) $5,000,000, to remain available until expended.October 1, 2010

children.heading: Receipt and acceptance
children.label: B Receipt and acceptance
children.text: The Secretary shall be entitled to receive, shall accept, and shall use to carry out subsection (a)(2) the funds transferred under subparagraph (A), without further appropriation.

children.heading: Additional funding
children.label: 2 Additional funding
children.text: In addition to amounts made available under paragraph (1), there are authorized to be appropriated to carry out subsection (a)(2) such sums as are necessary for fiscal year 1995 and each subsequent fiscal year. The Secretary shall carry out activities under subsection (a)(2), in addition to the activities funded under paragraph (1), to the extent provided for, and in such amounts as are provided for, in advance in appropriations Acts.

children.heading: Funding for education, training, or applied research or studies
children.label: 3 Funding for education, training, or applied research or studies
children.text: In addition to amounts made available under paragraphs (1) and (2), from amounts otherwise appropriated to the Secretary in discretionary appropriations, the Secretary may provide funds to any food service management institute established under subsection (a)(2) for projects specified by the Secretary that will contribute to implementing dietary or nutrition initiatives. Any additional funding under this subparagraph shall be provided noncompetitively in a separate cooperative agreement.

children.heading: Administrative training and technical assistance material
children.label: f Administrative training and technical assistance material
children.text: In collaboration with State educational agencies, local educational agencies, and school food authorities of varying sizes, the Secretary shall develop and distribute training and technical assistance material relating to the administration of school meals programs that are representative of the best management and administrative practices.

children.label: i
children.text: on , and , $3,000,000;October 1, 2004October 1, 2005

children.label: ii

children.label: iii
children.text: on , and every October 1 thereafter, $4,000,000.October 1, 2010

children.heading: Receipt and acceptance
children.label: B Receipt and acceptance
children.text: The Secretary shall be entitled to receive, shall accept, and shall use to carry out this subsection the funds transferred under subparagraph (A), without further appropriation.

children.heading: Availability of funds
children.label: C Availability of funds
children.text: Funds transferred under subparagraph (A) shall remain available until expended.

children.label: A
children.text: to provide training and technical assistance and material related to improving program integrity and administrative accuracy in school meals programs; and

children.label: B
children.text: to assist State educational agencies in reviewing the administrative practices of local educational agencies, to the extent determined by the Secretary.
